content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (from autonomous agent research) can be repurposed to prioritize which new unlabeled data gets written to long-term memory in cutting-edge 2024-2025 active learning agents, solving the bottleneck of finite memory capacity that plagues current SOTA active learning agents. Instead of active learning only selecting data to improve task model accuracy, it can simultaneously use intrinsic curiosity reward to select which data to retain in memory to enable future open-ended active learning.
